---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v3.png')
background-size: 105%
background-class: center
class: middle

.size45[**II. Policy Design Options**]

<br>

.size50[

**Today's Agenda**

Exploring the Success Stories

- Adaptive Governance
]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2024)
]]

???

## Prep for Class
1. Review submitted cases



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

.size70[**Paper 2**]

.size55[
Which of the four policy design options is the "right" choice for addressing your environmental problem?

- Analyze the pros and cons of each option for your chosen problem
]

???

Keep your eye on the next target: Paper 2.

- In that paper you will be asked to evaluate all four of the approaches we study in class from the perspective of your chosen environmental problem.

### Questions on the paper so far?

<br>

Today we analyze our final policy approach using your real world success cases.




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[**Assignment for Today**]

.size45[
Submit to Canvas a real world example of .textblue[**this approach**] being used to .textblue[**successfully**] address an environmental problem .textblue[**similar to the one in your project**].
]

.size45[
.textblue[**Present as an argument**]: This case shows that addressing environmental problem X can be done successfully using adaptive governance approaches.
]

???

Let's review all the cases for APA format.




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.center[.content-box-blue[.size45[This case shows that we can use adaptive governance to address environmental problem X.]]]

<br>

.size40[
- What aspect of your problem does this policy focus on?

- Which stakeholders does it target? How?

- What institutions support this policy?
]

???

Take five minutes to reflect on your argument and get ready to present it to other people as answers to these three questions.

- Problem: Specific, narrow aspect targeted by this policy?

- Stakeholders: What does it require them to do?

- Institutions: What rules or structures currently exists to make this possible?

### Questions on this?

<br>

Get to it!

<br>

Welcome to our environmental policy cocktail party!

- We will now take 25 minutes to mingle, present our cases and hear everybody elses' cases.

- I don't want to see groups of three!
    - Your job is to interact in pairs.

- I want pairs focused on hearing one case and presenting one case at a time.

### Questions?

Go!

*You mingle too.*
- This was super fun and you got to help steer students to being more specific, more precise




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size60[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of an .textblue[**adaptive governance**] approach to environmental problem-solving]]

???

Ok, at this point you've heard MANY different cases and hopefully gotten some time to reflect on your own case argument.

<br>

Now let's try to extract lessons from all of them.

- Everybody form groups of (aim for three groups to keep board space manageable) and spread out around the room.

- Groups: I'm going to ask you to compare and contrast the cases you heard today to help us extract general lessons for policy designers.




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

.size50[Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of an .textblue[**adaptive governance**] approach to environmental problem-solving]

<br>

.content-box-blue[.size55[What kinds of problem characteristics fit best with this policy design? What kinds do not?]]

???

Ok, groups, first prompt.

You just heard a bunch of cases where adaptive governance successfully addressed an environmental problem. 

From those cases I want your best advice for a policy designer:

*ON BOARD: 2 Columns*

- **PRESENT EACH and DISCUSS**



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

.size50[Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of an .textblue[**adaptive governance**] approach to environmental problem-solving]

<br>

.content-box-blue[.size55[What kinds of stakeholder characteristics fit best with this policy design and what kinds do not?]]

???

Based on the success cases, I want your best advice for a policy designer:

*ON BOARD: 2 Columns*

- **PRESENT EACH and DISCUSS**



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

.size50[Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of an .textblue[**adaptive governance**] approach to environmental problem-solving]

<br>

.content-box-blue[.size55[What kinds of institutional support is required to make this policy design successful?]]

???

Based on the success cases, I want your best advice for a policy designer:

*ON BOARD*

- **PRESENT EACH and DISCUSS**




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/13-1-Huerta_Murcia.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: bottom
class: bottom, center, inverse

.textwhite[.size60[**Would adaptive governance work well for your project?**]]

???

### Which principle would be easiest to implement as part of your design? Why?

<br>

### Which principle would be most difficult to implement as part of your design? Why?

<br>

### Could any of these work on their own for your project? Why or why not?




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

.size70[**Paper 2**]

.size55[
Which of the four policy design options is the "right" choice for addressing your environmental problem?

- Analyze the pros and cons of each option for your chosen problem
]

???

Next week we work on the paper!

In this paper you will be asked to evaluate all four of the approaches we study in class from the perspective of your chosen environmental problem.

<br>

**SLIDE**: To set us up for this paper...



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

.size70[**Assignment for Tuesday**]

.size45[
**Before class** submit a start to your evaluation of the four policy options from class. 
- Define your environmental problem

- What is one strong "pro" for using each of the policy options to address your environmental problem?
]

???

Each answer should be AT LEAST one complete sentence.


Next week we explore processes of policy-making and problem-solving.

### Questions on the assignment?
